title: Promote a public pkg/serveapi.Server wrapper so external modules can embed the daemon in-process (successor to ied)
description:
moonshine-go-ied (closed) extracted daemon assembly from cmd/moonshine/serve.go's package main into an importable internal/serve.Server + ServerConfig. This is real progress -- code inside this module (github.com/ghchinoy/moonshine-go) can now construct and run the daemon in-process with a custom AgentHandler/AudioSource, verified via internal/serve/server_test.go.

However, Server/ServerConfig live in internal/serve, so Go's internal/ visibility rule still blocks any module rooted outside github.com/ghchinoy/moonshine-go from importing them. A genuine third-party consumer (e.g. samples/go-cascade-faq, which deliberately uses a separate go.mod + replace directive to simulate exactly this) still cannot embed the daemon in-process -- it can only run as a separate process using pkg/serveapi over WS/gRPC.

Scoping a fix: promote a pkg/serveapi.Server (or similar) that wraps internal/serve.NewServer/Server -- likely a thin public struct/constructor in pkg/serveapi that delegates to the internal implementation (mirroring how internal/serve now aliases pkg/serveapi types the other direction, per 8uk). Needs a design pass: ServerConfig currently references internal/serve types directly (Hub, Transport, AgentHandler, Speaker are internal/serve's own types, not yet all aliased -- verify which of these already alias pkg/serveapi's public interfaces per 8uk and which don't) and internal/moonshine.Transcriber (which is fine, cgo-free) plus session-loop wiring that pulls in internal/audio for mic barge-in muting (SetMutedFunc type assertion) -- that cgo dependency needs to stay optional/interface-based so pkg/serveapi.Server itself doesn't force CGO_ENABLED=1 on a consumer that brings a non-mic AudioSource (e.g. serveapi.RemoteAudioSource, which is already cgo-free).

Not urgent -- samples/go-cascade-faq's separate-process shape is real, valuable, and already ships; this is about closing the remaining gap for consumers who specifically want in-process embedding (e.g. a single Go binary that is both the daemon and the custom agent, no IPC hop).

title: TranscriptEvent.Lines serializes as JSON null (not []) on the first event, tripping up non-Go clients
description:
Found building samples/python-listen (moonshine-go-a57). serveapi.TranscriptEvent.Lines lacks 'omitempty' (pkg/serveapi/event.go:30), so a nil Go slice (the case on the very first TranscriptEvent, before any line exists) marshals to JSON 'lines: null' rather than 'lines: []' or omitting the key. FinalizedLineIDs (line 38) already has omitempty and doesn't have this problem.

Python's common idiom payload.get('lines', []) does NOT protect against this -- the key IS present, just with value None, so it still raises TypeError on iteration. Every non-Go client (Python, JS, etc.) needs an explicit 'or []' / '?? []' fallback, which is a paper-cut most won't discover until it crashes on them (exactly what happened building this sample).

Low priority, easy fix: add 'omitempty' to Lines' json tag (matching FinalizedLineIDs' existing pattern) so the key is omitted entirely when empty, which every JSON client handles uniformly. Purely a wire-format nit, not a logic bug -- worked around in samples/python-listen/listen.py with '(payload.get("lines") or [])'.
close_reason: Added omitempty tag to TranscriptEvent.Lines in pkg/serveapi/event.go so nil lines slice is omitted instead of serializing as JSON null.

title: Swappable Transcriber backend (local-default, optional cloud STT) — hybrid future option
description: Lesson from the online STT landscape: hybrid is the honest end-state. Define a Transcriber backend interface so serve can use local Moonshine by default but optionally route to a cloud STT (e.g. for max-accuracy or exotic-language jobs), mirroring the swappable LLMClient pattern. MUST preserve the air-gapped guarantee: cloud STT is opt-in, OFF by default, and hard-disabled in air-gapped mode (raw audio must not leave the box unless explicitly allowed). Deliberate FUTURE option, not committed — this task is design + scoping first.
design: Architectural. Interface abstraction over the current internal/moonshine Transcriber; local impl stays default. Cloud backend is a separate opt-in impl gated by config, disabled under an air-gapped/no-egress flag. Contrast with the deployment-mode framing in opportunities.md §2: this adds a swappable EARS to match the swappable BRAIN, without weakening the 'audio dies at the mic' default. Keep native-free tests via a fake Transcriber.
acceptance_criteria: Design doc / interface proposal for a swappable Transcriber; local Moonshine remains the default; cloud backend (if implemented) is opt-in, off by default, and provably disabled in air-gapped mode; air-gapped guarantee documented and tested.

title: Domain vocabulary / keyterm boosting for regulated verticals
description: Lesson from Deepgram keyterm boosting + ElevenLabs: regulated verticals (medical drug names, legal case numbers, financial tickers) need domain-vocabulary biasing at recognition time. Investigate and, if upstream support lands, expose a keyterm/keyword-boost option on the transcriber. Complements (does not replace) the RAG story in opportunities.md.
design: OPEN QUESTION / genuine upstream caveat: moonshine-c-api.h's recognized-options list (log_ort_run, ort_providers, identify_speakers, diarization_*, spelling_model_path) does NOT include keyterm/keyword/vocab/hotword/boost. tokenizer.bin/vocab is the model's token mapping, not runtime biasing. This is NOT currently possible via the C API. FIRST STEP: confirm with upstream libmoonshine whether recognition-time vocabulary biasing is on their roadmap; do not implement a moonshine-go shim that pretends to boost. If unsupported, consider a post-hoc correction/spelling-fusion approach (spelling_model_path exists) as a partial alternative.
acceptance_criteria: Either: (a) upstream keyterm-boost support confirmed and exposed as a transcriber option with tests; or (b) documented finding that it is unsupported upstream, with the spelling-fusion alternative evaluated. No fake 'boosting' that doesn't actually bias recognition.

title: Adopt prebuilt Linux release asset once onnxruntime bundling + RPATH fix lands upstream
description:
moonshine-ai/moonshine now publishes prebuilt moonshine-voice-linux-x86_64.tar.gz assets on GitHub Releases (confirmed present as of v0.0.69, uploaded 2026-07-20), which would let moonshine-go skip building libmoonshine from source entirely on Linux -- no C++ toolchain, no git-lfs pull of the moonshine core checkout, just a curl+tar of a ~14MB archive.

NOT YET ACTIONABLE: verified via scripts/check-release-asset.sh that the current v0.0.69 Linux asset has two real gaps that make it unusable as-is for purego's dlopen-based loading:
1. libmoonshine.so dynamically depends on libonnxruntime.so.1 (confirmed via strings/otool dependency scan), but the archive does not include libonnxruntime.so.1 -- dlopen would fail to resolve it on any machine that doesn't already have it installed system-wide.
2. libmoonshine.so's runtime search path for that dependency is a hardcoded absolute path on the upstream maintainer's own build machine (/home/pete/moonshine-git/core/third-party/onnxruntime/lib/linux/x86_64), not a portable relative path (RPATH ) -- meaning even if libonnxruntime were bundled next to it in the same directory, the loader wouldn't find it there.

Reported upstream; maintainer (Pete Warden) confirmed both are 'straightforward improvements' and is investigating the necessary CI changes (separately from #1/macOS-Windows shared-library support, which has no ETA -- see the sibling issue for that).
design:
Once fixed upstream (verify with 'scripts/check-release-asset.sh linux-x86_64 <tag>' -- should report PASS on both the onnxruntime-bundled and portable-RPATH checks), the actual adoption work here is:

1. Decide where this fits relative to scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh (source-build path) -- likely a new script (e.g. scripts/fetch-libmoonshine.sh or a --prebuilt flag on the existing one) that downloads and extracts moonshine-voice-linux-x86_64.tar.gz for a pinned release tag into .moonshine/lib/, matching the same output layout build-libmoonshine.sh already produces (so internal/moonshine.ResolveLibPath doesn't need to change).
2. Establish the actual version pin: which moonshine-ai/moonshine release tag does a given moonshine-go commit/release trust for prebuilt assets? This should be an explicit, deliberately-bumped value (not 'whatever's latest at build time') -- e.g. a constant or a MOONSHINE_RELEASE_TAG file at the repo root, checked by both this fetch script and CI. Bump it only when actually verified compatible (HeaderVersion in internal/moonshine/lib.go, currently 20000, is the runtime compatibility floor this pin must not violate -- verify moonshine_get_version() from the fetched release still satisfies it).
3. This unblocks a real GH Actions release/build pipeline for Linux specifically (build+package the moonshine-go CLI binary + fetched libmoonshine.so + libonnxruntime.so into a downloadable artifact) without touching moonshine-ai's git-lfs bandwidth at all -- a GitHub Release download doesn't consume LFS quota the way a git clone of the LFS-heavy core checkout does.
4. macOS and Windows remain source-build-only (or manual) until the sibling issue (shared library support) is resolved upstream -- don't block this on those.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'scripts/check-release-asset.sh linux-x86_64 <pinned-tag>' passes all checks (shared lib present, onnxruntime bundled, portable rpath)
- A documented, deliberately-bumped version pin exists recording which moonshine-ai/moonshine release tag this repo trusts for prebuilt Linux assets
- A fetch mechanism exists that stages the downloaded libmoonshine.so + libonnxruntime.so into the same .moonshine/lib/ layout scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh already produces, verified via 'make smoke' passing against the fetched (not locally-built) library
close_reason: Created MOONSHINE_RELEASE_TAG pinned to v0.0.71, scripts/fetch-libmoonshine.sh, and make fetchlib Makefile target

title: Bind grapheme-to-phonemizer G2P API + moonshine_phonemes_to_speech; add 'tts --ipa'
description:
Two related, currently-fully-unbound pieces of moonshine's TTS C API, discovered together while reviewing an upstream pull (moonshine core commit 6a0439d 'Added phoneme to speech support in TTS'):

1. moonshine_phonemes_to_speech (NEW as of that commit): synthesizes speech directly from an IPA phoneme string, skipping grapheme-to-phoneme (G2P) conversion. Same synthesizer handle/options ('speed' etc.) as moonshine_text_to_speech. This is the 'second half' of a G2P-inspect-and-edit workflow -- lets a caller fix mispronunciation (e.g. proper nouns) by hand-editing IPA between G2P and vocoding.
2. moonshine_create_grapheme_to_phonemizer_from_memory / moonshine_text_to_phonemes / moonshine_free_grapheme_to_phonemizer (PRE-EXISTING, was already unbound before this pull -- not new, just discovered to be a gap while investigating #1): the G2P step itself, producing the IPA string moonshine_phonemes_to_speech (or the internal G2P moonshine_text_to_speech already does automatically) consumes.

Currently internal/moonshine has ZERO references to phonemes, IPA, text_to_phonemes, or grapheme_to_phonemizer anywhere -- confirmed via search. Binding only moonshine_phonemes_to_speech without also binding the G2P step would only support callers who hand-author raw IPA themselves; binding both gives the full inspect-and-edit workflow moonshine's own docs describe for this feature (see moonshine core's micro/neural-tts docs for the 'Reading, Pennsylvania' example of exactly this use case: default G2P misreads a place name, SynthesizeIpa/phonemes_to_speech with hand-corrected IPA fixes it).

MOONSHINE_HEADER_VERSION is unchanged (still 20000) by the commit that added this -- no version bump, no compatibility concern, just new unbound API surface.
design:
internal/moonshine/g2p.go (new file): ctypes + Go wrappers for the G2P chain. Confirmed both moonshine_create_grapheme_to_phonemizer_from_files AND ..._from_memory exist (mirror the transcriber/synthesizer pattern of supporting both -- LoadTranscriber uses from_files, NewSynthesizer uses from_files too via moonshine_create_tts_synthesizer_from_files with filenames=nil; prefer from_files for the initial binding, consistent with the rest of this package, add from_memory later only if a real need arises), moonshine_text_to_phonemes, and moonshine_free_grapheme_to_phonemizer. Mirror the pattern in tts.go (Synthesizer type) -- a Phonemizer type wrapping the handle with Close()/finalizer, a TextToPhonemes(text string) (string, error) method.

internal/moonshine/tts.go: add PhonemesToSpeech(phonemes string, opts ...Option) (Audio, error) method on the existing Synthesizer type (or a standalone function taking a synthesizer handle) wrapping moonshine_phonemes_to_speech -- same option/audio-decoding pattern as the existing Synthesize method.

cmd/moonshine/tts.go: add a --ipa flag (mutually exclusive with the positional text argument, similar to how neural-tts's own host CLI tts_cli does '--ipa' vs a positional text arg per the Micro investigation) that calls PhonemesToSpeech directly, skipping G2P. Consider also exposing text-to-phonemes standalone (e.g. 'moonshine tts --show-phonemes' or a separate small subcommand) so users can see default G2P output before deciding whether to hand-edit it -- needed for the edit workflow to be practically usable, not just phonemes-to-speech in isolation.

Docs: docs/user-guide.md's tts section gets an IPA override subsection with the Reading/Pennsylvania-style example; README docs list unaffected.
acceptance_criteria:
- internal/moonshine has ctypes + wrappers for the full G2P chain (NewPhonemizer/TextToPhonemes/Close) plus Synthesizer.PhonemesToSpeech -- DONE
- 'moonshine tts --ipa "..."' synthesizes audio directly from hand-written IPA, verified against kokoro_af_heart -- DONE
- 'moonshine tts --show-phonemes "..."' prints default G2P output (chose a flag, not a subcommand) -- DONE
- docs/user-guide.md documents the IPA override workflow with a real, verified mispronunciation-fixing example ("Reading, Pennsylvania": G2P's ɹˈidɪŋ -> hand-corrected ˈɹɛdɪŋ) -- DONE
- Bonus: mutual-exclusivity (--ipa + <text>) and missing-arg (--show-phonemes with no text) validation added and verified
- Full moonshinesmoke suite still passes

title: Add Intent Recognizer bindings
description: moonshine-c-api.h's INTENT RECOGNIZER section (moonshine_create_intent_recognizer, moonshine_register_intent/unregister_intent, moonshine_get_closest_intents, moonshine_calculate_intent_embedding, moonshine_get_intent_dependencies for its embedding-model manifest, e.g. embeddinggemma-300m) has no Go binding at all in internal/moonshine -- not in ctypes.go, lib.go, or any wrapper type. This is a wholly new subsystem (embedding-based intent matching against registered canonical phrases) with zero moonshine-go coverage. (Split out from the original combined issue; the diarization/word-timestamps flags half is now tracked separately as moonshine-go-f4a, P2, since that only needs new CLI flags over existing plumbing -- this issue is a much bigger net-new binding + CLI surface.)

A corresponding download path (uses moonshine_get_intent_dependencies, same DependencyManifest shape as STT) -- likely 'moonshine setup --intent-model ...' or a separate subcommand. A new CLI surface (e.g. 'moonshine intent register/match' or similar) -- needs its own UX scoping, distinct from transcribe/tts/live/setup/config.

title: Add 'moonshine mcp' subcommand exposing transcribe via MCP
description: Add an MCP server subcommand (moonshine mcp) using github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk, exposing a 'transcribe' tool over stdio for MCP-only hosts (Claude Desktop, IDE MCP integrations) that have no general shell/bash tool and thus cannot use the CLI directly. v1 scope is transcribe only -- tts/live are out of scope (live is a streaming mic session that doesn't map cleanly to a single request/response tool call).
design:
- New file cmd/moonshine/mcp.go (or internal/mcpserver package) wiring mcp.NewServer + mcp.AddTool for a 'transcribe' tool mirroring transcribeCmd's inputs (input path/gs:// URI, language, arch, providers) and transcribeResult's output shape (lines + stats), using Go struct tags for JSON schema per go-sdk conventions.
- loadLibrary() must happen lazily/once (sync.Once) on first tool call, not per-call like the CLI does.
- Add a transcriber cache keyed by (language, arch, providers) so repeated tool calls reuse an already-loaded model instead of reloading ONNX weights every call -- this is the main perf win over just shelling out to the CLI repeatedly from an MCP client.
- GCS input (gs://) reuses internal/gcsfetch; document that ADC must be valid in whatever environment launches the MCP server subprocess (host configs often strip shell env/gcloud state).
- Run via 'moonshine mcp' (stdio transport), added to rootCmd similar to other subcommands.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'moonshine mcp' subcommand starts an MCP stdio server
- 'transcribe' tool callable via mcp go-sdk test client (see go-sdk README client example) round-trips a local wav file to lines+stats matching CLI's --json output shape
- gs:// input works
- second call with same language/arch does not reload the model (verify via timing or a load-count counter in tests)
- README/docs mention the new subcommand and required env vars for MCP host configs

title: Track upstream shared library (dylib/dll) support for macOS/Windows releases
description:
Watch-only, no ETA -- this is explicitly NOT scoped/actionable work yet, just a placeholder so the open question doesn't get lost.

moonshine-ai/moonshine's prebuilt release assets (moonshine-voice-macos-arm64.tar.gz, moonshine-voice-windows-x86_64.tar.gz) currently only ship STATIC libraries (libmoonshine.a on macOS, moonshine.lib on Windows), confirmed via scripts/check-release-asset.sh against v0.0.69. purego (this project's FFI mechanism) requires a dynamically loadable shared library (dlopen/.dylib on macOS, LoadLibrary/.dll on Windows) -- a static archive can't be dlopen'd, so these two platforms' prebuilt assets are not usable for moonshine-go regardless of any onnxruntime-bundling/RPATH fix (that's the sibling Linux issue, moonshine-go-glg, which IS on a 'straightforward' track).

Reported upstream; maintainer (Pete Warden) response: 'investigating how to update the CI testing to cover both static and dynamic libraries' -- i.e. this requires him to stand up a whole second build+test matrix (dual static/shared artifacts), not just flip a CMake flag, since MOONSHINE_BUILD_SHARED=ON changes how the existing Swift/iOS (macOS) and C++ example app (Windows) consumers link against it, and he doesn't yet have CI coverage to catch regressions in that path.

Worth noting for prioritization: macOS is the platform this project's own dev machine and CI would benefit from most (this repo's local dev loop runs on darwin/arm64), but it's on the slower/uncertain track here, unlike Linux.
design:
No design yet -- there's nothing to build until upstream ships a shared macOS/Windows artifact. When it does:
1. Re-run scripts/check-release-asset.sh macos-arm64/windows-x86_64 <tag> to verify it's actually usable (shared lib + bundled onnxruntime + portable search path, same three checks as the Linux issue).
2. If it passes, this becomes essentially the same adoption work as moonshine-go-glg, extended to the other two platforms -- extend whatever fetch mechanism gets built there rather than designing a separate one.
3. Until then, macOS/Windows continue to require scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh (source build from a local moonshine checkout) as the only path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Re-check periodically (or when Pete announces movement) via 'scripts/check-release-asset.sh macos-arm64 <tag>' and 'scripts/check-release-asset.sh windows-x86_64 <tag>'
- Once either passes, convert this into real scoped work (or fold into moonshine-go-glg's fetch mechanism) rather than leaving it as a watch-only placeholder
